message:
Fix bugs in installer library modules

cli_tools.sh:
- Remove unused variable target_home in _cli_run_as_user()

smoke_test.sh:
- Add NONCRITICAL_PASS counter for non-critical checks
- Reset all counters at start of run_smoke_test() to handle multiple runs
- Add _smoke_info() helper for non-critical passes
- Fix _check_agent_mail, _check_stack_tools, _check_postgres to use
  _smoke_info instead of _smoke_pass (prevents inflating critical count)
- Fix inconsistent spacing in warning emoji output

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","title":"EPIC: CI/CD & Quality Gates","description":"# Epic: CI/CD & Quality Gates

## Overview
Set up comprehensive CI/CD pipeline to ensure quality and enable confident deployments.

## Background & Reasoning
A project of this scope needs automated quality gates:
- Catch bugs before they reach users
- Verify installer actually works on real Ubuntu
- Ensure website builds correctly
- Maintain code quality standards

## CI Components

### 1. Code Quality
- ESLint for TypeScript/JavaScript
- Prettier for formatting
- shellcheck for bash scripts
- TypeScript type checking

### 2. Website Tests
- Unit tests for components
- Build verification
- Lighthouse CI for performance

### 3. Installer Tests
- Spin Ubuntu VM (GitHub Actions runner or cloud)
- Run full install.sh
- Execute acfs doctor
- Verify all tools present

### 4. Integration Tests
- Manifest parsing
- Generator outputs
- Doctor check coverage

## GitHub Actions Workflows

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
- lint-and-type-check
- test-website
- test-installer-ubuntu-25
- shellcheck-scripts
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All PRs require passing CI
- [ ] Installer tested on real Ubuntu
- [ ] Website build verified
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all .sh files
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] Deployment preview for PRs

## Considerations
- Keep CI fast (< 10 min for most checks)
- Installer test can be slower (separate workflow)
- Use caching aggressively
- Matrix test multiple Ubuntu versions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:08.720517-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:08.720517-05:00","labels":["ci","epic"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for website CI","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Website CI

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow for website quality gates.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/website.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. lint-and-typecheck
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run lint
- run: bun run type-check
```

### 2. build
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run build
```

### 3. test (if tests exist)
```yaml
- run: bun test
```

### 4. lighthouse (optional)
```yaml
- uses: treosh/lighthouse-ci-action@v10
  with:
    urls: |
      http://localhost:3000/
    uploadArtifacts: true
```

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests to main

## Caching
```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v3
  with:
    path: ~/.bun/install/cache
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs on every PR
- [ ] Lint + typecheck required to pass
- [ ] Build verification
- [ ] Caching for speed
- [ ] Clear failure messages","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:08.308979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:48:52.669536-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:48:52.669536-05:00","close_reason":"Created GitHub Actions workflow for website CI. Fixed barrel import issues causing SSG build failures by switching to direct component imports. All lint, type-check, and build steps now pass.","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:08.310651-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for installer testing","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Installer Testing

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow that tests the installer on real Ubuntu.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/installer.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. shellcheck
Fast job that runs on every PR:
```yaml
- uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
  with:
    scandir: './scripts'
    additional_files: 'install.sh'
```

### 2. test-ubuntu-25
Full installer test (may be slow):
```yaml
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
container:
  image: ubuntu:25.04
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4
  - name: Run installer
    run: |
      bash install.sh --yes --mode vibe
  - name: Run doctor
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "acfs doctor"
  - name: Verify key tools
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "ntm --help"
      su - ubuntu -c "ubs --help"
      # ... etc
```

### 3. test-ubuntu-24
Same but on 24.04 LTS for compatibility.

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests touching install.sh or scripts/

## Matrix Testing
```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    ubuntu: ['24.04', '25.04']
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all scripts
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 25.04
- [ ] All tools verified after install
- [ ] acfs doctor exits 0","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.687302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.687302-05:00","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.688994-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","title":"EPIC: Onboarding TUI","description":"# Epic: Onboarding TUI

## Overview
Build the interactive `onboard` command that teaches users Linux basics and the ACFS workflow through hands-on missions.

## Background & Reasoning
Documentation fails beginners. Interactive learning succeeds. The onboard TUI:
- Teaches by doing, not reading
- Builds muscle memory for key commands
- Introduces tools progressively
- Can be re-run anytime as a refresher

## 7 Missions

### Mission 0: Welcome
- What you now have
- The VPS as playground (safe to break, just re-run ACFS)
- Overview of installed tools

### Mission 1: Linux Navigation
- pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, rm
- /home/ubuntu vs /data/projects
- Navigation shortcuts (dev, proj, p aliases)

### Mission 2: SSH & Persistence
- What SSH is
- Why tmux matters (connection drops, tmux lives)
- Basic attach/detach

### Mission 3: tmux Basics
- Create/attach/detach sessions
- Split panes
- Copy mode basics

### Mission 4: Agent Commands
- cc (Claude), cod (Codex), gmi (Gemini)
- First login flow
- `caam backup` right after login

### Mission 5: NTM Command Center
- ntm deps -v (verify)
- ntm tutorial
- ntm spawn demo --cc=2 --cod=1
- ntm send/broadcast

### Mission 6: NTM Command Palette
- Show vendored command_palette.md
- Searchable prompt list
- "Send to all agents" feature

### Mission 7: The Flywheel Loop
- Plan in Beads → bv
- Coordinate via Agent Mail
- Run agents via NTM
- Search via cass
- Build memory via cm
- Scan with ubs
- Optional guardrails via slb

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 7 missions implemented
- [ ] Can be re-run safely anytime
- [ ] Works offline (except optional updates)
- [ ] Takes < 30 minutes to complete all
- [ ] User feels confident to start coding

## Technical Approach
- Shell script or simple Go/Rust binary
- Uses gum or similar for TUI elements
- Stores progress in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:25.187518-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:25.187518-05:00","labels":["epic","onboard"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","title":"Create onboard TUI shell script","description":"# Task: Create Onboard TUI Shell Script

## Description
Build the interactive onboarding TUI that teaches users the ACFS workflow.

## Location
Install to: `~/.local/bin/onboard`

## UX Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Welcome to ACFS Onboarding                │
│                                                     │
│  Choose a lesson:                                   │
│                                                     │
│  [1] Welcome & Overview                ✓ completed  │
│  [2] Linux Navigation (5 min)          ● in progress│
│  [3] SSH & Persistence                              │
│  [4] tmux Basics                                    │
│  [5] Agent Commands (cc, cod, gmi)                  │
│  [6] NTM Command Center                             │
│  [7] NTM Prompt Palette                             │
│  [8] The Flywheel Loop                              │
│                                                     │
│  [q] Quit  [r] Restart from beginning               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Pure Bash + gum
Use charmbracelet/gum for TUI elements:
```bash
gum choose "Welcome" "Linux Navigation" "SSH & Persistence" ...
```

### Option B: Simple menu
Plain bash with read:
```bash
while true; do
    clear
    echo "ACFS Onboarding"
    echo "1) Welcome"
    echo "2) Linux Navigation"
    ...
    read -p "Choose: " choice
done
```

## Lesson Format
Each lesson:
1. Brief explanation
2. Command to try
3. Expected output
4. Checkpoint/quiz

## Progress Tracking
Store in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json:
```json
{
  "completed": [1, 2, 3],
  "current": 4,
  "started_at": "2025-12-19T..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 lessons implemented
- [ ] Progress saved between runs
- [ ] Can be re-run anytime
- [ ] Works over SSH
- [ ] Clear and beginner-friendly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","labels":["onboard","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.969656-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","title":"Set up bun workspaces for monorepo","description":"# Task: Set up Bun Workspaces

## Description
Configure bun workspaces in the root package.json to manage:
- apps/web (Next.js wizard website)
- packages/manifest (manifest utilities)
- packages/onboard (onboard TUI)

## Technical Details
Root package.json should include:
```json
{
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"]
}
```

Each workspace needs its own package.json with appropriate name/scripts.

## Reasoning
Bun workspaces allow:
- Shared dependencies across packages
- Single lockfile (bun.lock)
- Easy cross-package imports
- Consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root package.json with workspaces config
- [ ] Each workspace has valid package.json
- [ ] `bun install` works from root
- [ ] Cross-package imports resolve correctly

## Dependencies
Depends on directory structure being created first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.61383-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","close_reason":"Root bun workspace set up with apps/* and packages/* workspaces","labels":["infrastructure","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.616805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh)

## Overview
Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment.

## Background & Reasoning
This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must:
- Work when run as root (common VPS default)
- Work when run as ubuntu with sudo
- Be idempotent (safe to run twice)
- Handle connection drops and resume
- Log everything for debugging

## Design Philosophy
**Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction.
**Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work.
**Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs.

## Installer Phases
1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x)
2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers
3. Base packages (apt)
4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins)
5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep)
6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases)
7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail)
8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes
- [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything
- [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified
- [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green
- [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log

## Considerations
- Always prefer upstream installers when available
- Verify each tool after install
- Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions
- Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","close_reason":"All 12 sub-tasks completed. VPS installer fully modularized with libraries for: logging, OS detection, user normalization, shell setup, CLI tools, language runtimes, coding agents, Dicklesworthstone stack, cloud/database tools, smoke test, and canonical zshrc.","labels":["epic","installer"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library

## Description
Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output.

## Functions

```bash
log_step()    # [1/8] Step description (blue)
log_detail()  # Indented detail (gray)
log_success() # ✔ Success message (green)
log_warn()    # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow)
log_error()   # ✖ Error message (red)
log_debug()   # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1
```

## Requirements
- All output to stderr (keep stdout clean)
- Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors)
- Support --verbose flag (show debug)
- Timestamps in log file
- ANSI color codes with fallback

## Log File
Write all output to:
- `/var/log/acfs/install.log`
- `~/.acfs/logs/install.<timestamp>.log`

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/logging.sh

ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log"
ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}"
ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}"

log_step() {
    local step="$1"
    local msg="$2"
    echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All log functions implemented
- [ ] Colors work in terminal
- [ ] --quiet suppresses output
- [ ] --verbose shows debug
- [ ] Log file written with timestamps
- [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.9","title":"Create clean canonical acfs.zshrc","description":"# Task: Create Clean Canonical acfs.zshrc

## Description
Create the production-ready zshrc that ACFS deploys to VPS.

## File: acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc

## Requirements
Based on user's original zshrc but:
- Remove duplicate PATH/Bun blocks
- Remove duplicate aliases
- Use safe chpwd hook instead of overriding cd()
- Fix atuin path before init ordering
- Conditional tool aliases (only if tool exists)
- Support user overrides via ~/.zshrc.local

## Structure

```bash
# ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc
# ACFS canonical zsh config (managed)

# --- SSH guard ---
# Prevent weird terminal settings over SSH

# --- Paths (early) ---
# ~/.local/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, go, bun, atuin

# --- Oh My Zsh ---
# Theme: powerlevel10k
# Plugins: git, autosuggestions, syntax-highlighting, etc.

# --- Editor preference ---
# vim over SSH, nvim locally

# --- Modern CLI aliases ---
# Only set if tool exists
# lsd/eza, bat, fd, rg, dust, btop, nvim, lazygit

# --- Git aliases ---
# gs, gd, gdc, gp, gpu, gco, gcm, gca, gb, glog

# --- Docker aliases ---
# dc, dps, dpsa, dimg, dex

# --- Directory shortcuts ---
# dev, proj, dots, p

# --- Custom functions ---
# mkcd, extract

# --- Safe ls after cd ---
# Using chpwd hook, not overriding cd()

# --- Tool settings ---
# UV_LINK_MODE, etc.

# --- Tool initializations ---
# cargo, atuin, zoxide, direnv, fzf

# --- Agent aliases ---
# cc, cod, gmi (dangerous mode enabled)

# --- Keybindings ---
# Ctrl/Alt+Arrow for word movement
# Home/End keys
# Force atuin Ctrl-R
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicate code
- [ ] All tools alias only if present
- [ ] chpwd hook works correctly
- [ ] Atuin Ctrl-R works
- [ ] Local overrides supported
- [ ] Passes shellcheck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:40.519614-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:59:34.896944-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:59:34.896944-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed shellcheck warnings, added directives for zsh-specific variables. File already met all requirements: no duplicates, conditional aliases, safe chpwd hook, proper atuin ordering, local overrides support.","labels":["config","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.9","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:40.521482-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5vz","title":"Add installer security verification","description":"# Task: Add Installer Security Verification

## Description
Make the curl|bash pattern more trustworthy.

## Features

### 1. Checksum Verification
Before running sub-installers, verify checksums:
```bash
verify_checksum() {
    local url="$1"
    local expected_sha256="$2"
    local content=$(curl -fsSL "$url")
    local actual=$(echo "$content" | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
    if [[ "$actual" != "$expected_sha256" ]]; then
        log_error "Checksum mismatch for $url"
        exit 1
    fi
    echo "$content"
}
```

### 2. HTTPS Enforcement
All URLs must be HTTPS. Fail on HTTP.

### 3. --print Mode
Show exactly what will be downloaded:
```bash
install.sh --print
# Outputs:
# Will download:
# - https://bun.sh/install (sha256: abc123...)
# - https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh (sha256: def456...)
# ...
```

### 4. Source Links
Website shows link to install.sh source on GitHub.

### 5. Changelog
What changed since last version.

## Checksums File
```yaml
# checksums.yaml
installers:
  bun: "abc123..."
  uv: "def456..."
  # etc
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksum verification for key installers
- [ ] HTTPS enforced
- [ ] --print mode works

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","title":"EPIC: acfs doctor & Update Commands","description":"# Epic: acfs doctor & Update Commands

## Overview
Build the diagnostic and update commands that help users verify and maintain their ACFS installation.

## Background & Reasoning
Beginners don't debug - they need clear "is it working?" feedback and simple fixes.

### acfs doctor
Single command to verify entire installation:
- Identity (user ubuntu, passwordless sudo)
- Shell setup (zsh, plugins, theme)
- All tools installed and working
- Clear PASS/FAIL/WARN for each check

### acfs update
Keep everything current:
- apt update + upgrade
- Update bun globals (codex, gemini, vercel, etc.)
- Optionally re-run tool installers

## Doctor Output Spec

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Dicklesworthstone stack
  PASS ✅ ntm (available)
  PASS ✅ slb (available)
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs structured data for website integration:
```json
{
  "checks": [
    {"id": "identity.user_is_ubuntu", "status": "pass", ...}
  ],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] acfs doctor checks all ~40 tools
- [ ] Each check has stable ID for website mapping
- [ ] Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any fail
- [ ] --json mode for automation
- [ ] acfs update works without interaction
- [ ] Clear fix suggestions for each failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:55.795457-05:00","labels":["epic","tooling"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","title":"Implement acfs doctor command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs doctor Command

## Description
Build the diagnostic command that verifies the entire ACFS installation.

## Location
Install to: `~/.acfs/bin/acfs`
Subcommand: `acfs doctor`

## Output Format

```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
User: ubuntu ✅
Mode: vibe (passwordless sudo: enabled) ✅
OS: Ubuntu 25.x ✅
Workspace: /data/projects ✅

Shell
  PASS ✅ zsh installed (zsh 5.x)
  PASS ✅ oh-my-zsh installed
  ...

Summary: 34 passed, 0 failed, 1 warning
Next: run `onboard`
```

## Check Structure

Each check has:
- id: Stable identifier for website mapping
- label: Human-readable name
- status: pass | warn | fail
- details: Version or extra info
- fix: Command to fix (on failure)

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash

run_check() {
    local id="$1"
    local label="$2"
    local check_cmd="$3"
    local fix_cmd="$4"
    
    if eval "$check_cmd" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "PASS ✅ $label"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"pass\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
    else
        echo "FAIL ❌ $label"
        echo "      Fix: $fix_cmd"
        echo "{\"id\":\"$id\",\"status\":\"fail\",\"fix\":\"$fix_cmd\"}" >> "$JSON_OUTPUT"
        ((FAIL_COUNT++))
    fi
}

# Run all checks
run_check "identity.user_is_ubuntu" "Logged in as ubuntu" \
    "[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]" \
    "sudo -u ubuntu bash"
```

## JSON Mode
`acfs doctor --json` outputs:
```json
{
  "acfs_version": "0.1.0",
  "checks": [...],
  "summary": {"pass": 34, "warn": 1, "fail": 0}
}
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: All pass (warnings OK)
- 1: Any failure
- 2: Doctor crashed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ~40 checks implemented
- [ ] Stable check IDs
- [ ] --json mode works
- [ ] Correct exit codes
- [ ] Fix suggestions on failure
- [ ] Summary at end","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.266691-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.266691-05:00","labels":["task","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:41.268261-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.2","title":"Implement acfs update command","description":"# Task: Implement acfs update Command

## Description
Create command to update all ACFS-installed components.

## What It Updates

### System
```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y && sudo apt autoremove -y
```

### Bun Globals
```bash
bun install -g @openai/codex@latest
bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest
bun install -g wrangler@latest
bun install -g supabase@latest
bun install -g vercel@latest
```

### Claude Code
```bash
claude update
```

### Dicklesworthstone Stack
- Check for updates to each tool
- Re-run installers if --force flag

## Flags
- --apt-only: Only system packages
- --agents-only: Only coding agents
- --stack-only: Only Dicklesworthstone tools
- --force: Re-run all installers even if up-to-date

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Updates apt packages
- [ ] Updates bun globals
- [ ] Updates Claude Code
- [ ] Optional stack update
- [ ] Progress output
- [ ] Summary of what was updated","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:14.55906-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:14.55906-05:00","labels":["task","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:14.560845-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","title":"Create monorepo directory structure","description":"# Task: Create Monorepo Directory Structure

## Description
Set up the complete directory structure as specified in AGENTS.md:
- apps/web/ - Next.js 16 wizard website
- packages/manifest/ - Manifest parser and generators
- packages/installer/ - Installer helper scripts
- packages/onboard/ - Onboard TUI source
- acfs/ - Config files copied to ~/.acfs on VPS
- scripts/lib/ - Installer library functions
- scripts/providers/ - VPS provider guides
- scripts/sync/ - Sync scripts for vendored content
- tests/vm/ - VM-based installer tests

## Technical Details
- Use bun workspaces for packages
- Ensure proper .gitkeep files for empty dirs
- Add appropriate README.md in each major directory

## Reasoning
A clear, well-documented structure helps both humans and AI agents navigate the codebase efficiently. The structure is designed to separate concerns:
- Website is in apps/ (deployable to Vercel)
- Reusable packages in packages/
- VPS-side configs in acfs/
- Build/install tooling in scripts/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All directories created per AGENTS.md
- [ ] README.md in each major directory explaining its purpose
- [ ] .gitkeep in empty directories

## Considerations
- Don't create files that aren't needed yet
- Structure should be obvious to someone new to the project","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:23.040049-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.559741-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.559741-05:00","close_reason":"Monorepo directory structure created: apps/web, packages/{installer,manifest,onboard}","labels":["infrastructure","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fac","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:23.041903-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fac","title":"EPIC: Project Foundation & Infrastructure","description":"# Epic: Project Foundation & Infrastructure

## Overview
Set up the foundational infrastructure for the ACFS project, including repo structure, tooling, and development environment.

## Background & Reasoning
Before any feature development can begin, we need a solid foundation:
- Proper monorepo structure for website + installer + onboard
- Consistent tooling (bun, TypeScript, linting)
- CI/CD pipeline for quality gates
- Documentation standards

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All directory structure matches AGENTS.md spec
- [ ] bun workspace configured properly
- [ ] CI runs on every PR
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all bash scripts

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation

## Considerations
- Keep structure minimal initially, add complexity only when needed
- Prefer convention over configuration
- Make it easy for agents to understand and navigate

## Relationship to Project Goals
This enables all subsequent development by providing a consistent, well-documented codebase structure that both humans and AI agents can work with effectively.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:19:59.763401-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.701537-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.701537-05:00","close_reason":"Foundation complete: directory structure, install.sh, library scripts, configs all created","labels":["epic","infrastructure"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","title":"EPIC: Module Manifest System","description":"# Epic: Module Manifest System

## Overview
Create the module manifest system (acfs.manifest.yaml) that serves as single source of truth for all installed tools.

## Background & Reasoning
Without a manifest, we'd have:
- Scattered install logic
- No way to generate doctor checks automatically
- Website and installer out of sync
- Hard to add/update tools

The manifest provides:
- Canonical list of all tools
- Install commands
- Verify commands
- Dependencies between modules
- Metadata for website display

## Manifest Structure

```yaml
version: 1
name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
id: acfs

defaults:
  user: ubuntu
  workspace_root: /data/projects
  mode: vibe

modules:
  - id: stack.ntm
    description: Named tmux manager (agent cockpit)
    category: stack
    priority: 1
    install:
      - curl -fsSL https://... | bash
    verify:
      - ntm --help
    aliases:
      - ntm
    docs_url: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm
```

## Generated Outputs
1. **Website**: Tools checklist on status page
2. **Installer**: Module installation scripts
3. **Doctor**: Check definitions with IDs
4. **Documentation**: Auto-generated tool reference

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Complete manifest covering all 40+ tools
- [ ] Parser library in packages/manifest
- [ ] Generator for website content
- [ ] Generator for installer modules
- [ ] Generator for doctor checks
- [ ] CI validates manifest syntax

## Considerations
- Keep YAML human-readable
- Use consistent IDs across the project
- Document the schema thoroughly
- Version the manifest format","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:39.336054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:39.336054-05:00","labels":["epic","manifest"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","title":"Create acfs.manifest.yaml with all modules","description":"# Task: Create Complete Module Manifest

## Description
Create the canonical manifest file with all 40+ tool definitions.

## File: acfs.manifest.yaml

## Structure

```yaml
version: 1
name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
id: acfs

defaults:
  user: ubuntu
  workspace_root: /data/projects
  mode: vibe
  
categories:
  - id: base
    name: System Fundamentals
    priority: 1
  - id: shell
    name: Shell Environment
    priority: 2
  # ... etc

modules:
  # Base system
  - id: base.curl
    description: HTTP client
    category: base
    install: [sudo apt-get install -y curl]
    verify: [curl --version]
    
  # ... 40+ modules
```

## Module Categories

1. **base** - System packages (curl, git, jq, etc.)
2. **users** - User/permissions setup
3. **shell** - zsh, oh-my-zsh, plugins, theme
4. **cli** - Modern CLI tools (lsd, bat, fzf, etc.)
5. **lang** - Language runtimes (bun, uv, rust, go)
6. **tools** - Dev tools (atuin, tmux, ast-grep)
7. **db** - Databases (postgres18)
8. **cloud** - Cloud CLIs (vault, wrangler, supabase, vercel)
9. **agents** - Coding agents (claude, codex, gemini)
10. **stack** - Dicklesworthstone tools (8 tools)

## Each Module Contains
- id: Stable identifier
- description: Short description
- category: Category reference
- install: List of install commands
- verify: List of verify commands
- aliases: Optional shell aliases
- docs_url: Documentation link
- dependencies: Other module IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 40+ modules defined
- [ ] Valid YAML syntax
- [ ] All verify commands documented
- [ ] Dependencies specified
- [ ] Categories organized logically","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.851933-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.851933-05:00","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.853698-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.2","title":"Build manifest parser library (packages/manifest)","description":"# Task: Build Manifest Parser Library

## Description
Create a TypeScript library to parse and work with acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Location
`packages/manifest/`

## Exports

```typescript
// Parse manifest
export function parseManifest(yamlPath: string): Manifest;

// Types
export interface Manifest {
  version: number;
  name: string;
  id: string;
  defaults: ManifestDefaults;
  categories: Category[];
  modules: Module[];
}

export interface Module {
  id: string;
  description: string;
  category: string;
  install: string[];
  verify: string[];
  aliases?: string[];
  docs_url?: string;
  dependencies?: string[];
}

// Utilities
export function getModulesByCategory(manifest: Manifest, category: string): Module[];
export function getModuleDependencies(manifest: Manifest, moduleId: string): Module[];
export function validateManifest(manifest: Manifest): ValidationResult;
```

## Features
- YAML parsing with proper error messages
- Schema validation
- Dependency cycle detection
- Type-safe access

## Dependencies
- yaml (parsing)
- zod (validation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses valid manifest
- [ ] Rejects invalid manifest with clear errors
- [ ] Type definitions exported
- [ ] Utilities work correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.533183-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.533183-05:00","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:23.53483-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.3","title":"Create manifest-to-installer generator","description":"# Task: Manifest-to-Installer Generator

## Description
Generate installer module scripts from acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Purpose
The manifest is the single source of truth. The installer shouldn't
duplicate this information - it should be generated.

## What It Generates

### For each module category, generate:
```bash
# scripts/generated/install_<category>.sh
# Auto-generated from acfs.manifest.yaml - DO NOT EDIT

install_base() {
    log_step "Installing base.curl"
    sudo apt-get install -y curl
    verify "curl --version"
    
    log_step "Installing base.git"
    # ... etc
}
```

### For doctor checks:
```bash
# scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh
CHECKS=(
    "identity.user_is_ubuntu|Logged in as ubuntu|[[ \$(whoami) == 'ubuntu' ]]"
    # ... etc
)
```

## Implementation
- packages/manifest/generate.ts
- Reads acfs.manifest.yaml
- Outputs to scripts/generated/

## CI Integration
- Run generator on manifest changes
- Fail if generated files differ (ensure they're committed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Generator script works
- [ ] Installer modules generated
- [ ] Doctor checks generated
- [ ] CI validates generated files","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.206152-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.206152-05:00","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:38.208032-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hii","title":"Create VERSION file and version management","description":"# Task: Create VERSION File and Version Management

## Description
Set up version management for ACFS releases.

## VERSION File
Root of repo: `VERSION`
```
0.1.0
```

## Version Format
Semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to installer
- MINOR: New features
- PATCH: Bug fixes

## Version Usage

### In installer
```bash
ACFS_VERSION=$(curl -fsSL .../VERSION)
```

### In acfs doctor
```
ACFS Doctor v0.1.0
```

### In website
Show version in footer

### In manifest
```yaml
version: 1
acfs_version: 0.1.0
```

## Release Process
1. Update VERSION file
2. Update CHANGELOG.md
3. Tag release: `git tag v0.1.0`
4. Push: `git push --tags`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VERSION file created
- [ ] All components read version correctly
- [ ] Version shown in doctor output
- [ ] Version shown on website","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:50.115466-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:50.115466-05:00","labels":["infrastructure","task"]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette

## Description
Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding.

## Background
The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy.

## Implementation

### Vendor Location
`acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Sync Script
`scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Sync NTM command palette from upstream

SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md"
DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md"

curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST"
```

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vqr","title":"Create SSH troubleshooting guide","description":"# Task: Create SSH Troubleshooting Guide

## Description
Comprehensive guide for common SSH connection failures.

## Location
- Website: Embedded in wizard step 6
- Installer: ~/.acfs/docs/ssh_troubleshooting.md

## Common Issues Covered

### Permission denied (publickey)
**Symptoms:** `Permission denied (publickey)`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong key file specified
2. Key not added to VPS
3. Wrong username

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Verify key exists
ls -la ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519*

# Try with verbose output
ssh -v -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP
```

### Connection refused
**Symptoms:** `Connection refused`
**Causes:**
1. SSH service not running
2. Firewall blocking port 22
3. Wrong IP address

**Fixes:**
- Check IP is correct
- VPS provider may need firewall rule

### Connection timeout
**Symptoms:** `Connection timed out`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong IP
2. VPS not fully booted
3. Network issue

**Fixes:**
- Wait 2-3 minutes for VPS to boot
- Verify IP in provider dashboard

### Host key verification failed
**Symptoms:** `Host key verification failed`
**Causes:** 
- VPS was recreated with same IP
- Man-in-the-middle warning

**Fixes:**
```bash
ssh-keygen -R IP_ADDRESS
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All common issues documented
- [ ] Clear cause → fix structure
- [ ] Commands are copy-paste ready
- [ ] Integrated in website step 6","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:03.569312-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:30:24.161611-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:30:24.161611-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive SSH troubleshooting guide at acfs/docs/ssh_troubleshooting.md covering all common issues","labels":["documentation","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","title":"EPIC: Website Wizard MVP","description":"# Epic: Website Wizard MVP

## Overview
Build the step-by-step wizard website that guides beginners from "I have a laptop" to "paste the curl|bash command".

## Background & Reasoning
The wizard is the user's first touchpoint with ACFS. It must:
- Feel premium and trustworthy
- Never overwhelm with jargon
- Make every step feel achievable
- Handle both Mac and Windows users
- Guide through VPS provider selection

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router
- Bun runtime
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- Vercel hosting + Cloudflare caching

## Key Design Principles
1. **Single action per screen** - One button or one command
2. **Copy button on every command** - Reduce friction
3. **"I did it" checkbox** - Gates "Next" button
4. **Collapsible troubleshooting** - Don't clutter but be ready

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 10 wizard steps implemented
- [ ] OS-aware command rendering
- [ ] Mobile responsive
- [ ] Lighthouse score > 90
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)

## Relationship to Project Goals
The wizard is the "front door" to ACFS. A confused or frustrated user at this stage will never reach the VPS installer. Excellence here directly enables the 10-minute promise.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:25:29.453083-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:25:29.453083-05:00","close_reason":"All 15 wizard steps implemented and verified. Epic complete.","labels":["epic","website"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.1","title":"Initialize Next.js 16 App Router project","description":"# Task: Initialize Next.js 16 App Router Project

## Description
Set up the Next.js 16 project in apps/web/ with:
- App Router structure
- TypeScript configuration
- Tailwind CSS
- shadcn/ui components

## Technical Details
```bash
cd apps/web
bunx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir
bunx shadcn-ui@latest init
```

## Configuration
- Use Bun as package manager
- Configure for Vercel deployment
- Set up path aliases (@/components, @/lib)
- Configure next.config.js for optimal settings

## Reasoning
Next.js 16 with App Router provides:
- Modern React Server Components
- Automatic code splitting
- Optimal performance defaults
- Easy Vercel deployment

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Next.js 16 project created
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled
- [ ] Tailwind CSS working
- [ ] shadcn/ui initialized
- [ ] bun run dev works
- [ ] bun run build succeeds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:18.88745-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","close_reason":"Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, and turbopack.
